title: solidago virgaurea subsp. stenophylla
intro: Solidago virgaurea subsp. stenophylla, also known as stiff-leaved goldenrod, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the aster family (Asteraceae) that is native to North America. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are thought to be due to the presence of flavonoids and terpenoids in its leaves and flowers. The health benefits of Solidago virgaurea subsp. stenophylla include reducing inflammation and improving respiratory health, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for colds and flu. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections.
